title: Planning State Hygiene Followthrough
goal: Implement the fresh package dogfooding issues #332 through #336 end-to-end.
Keep the shipped package provider-, repo-, agent-, tool-, and language-agnostic.
Commit and push after coherent milestones and close issues only after proof.
non_goals: Do not assume GitHub in shipped package behavior.
Do not close #230 without a fresh product-compression slice.

intent.outcome: Planning package surfaces make stale state, archive refusal correction, compact-state dogfooding, and provider-agnostic reconciliation cheaper and harder to misuse.
intent.constraints: No GitHub-specific core package assumptions; root planning stays authoritative; local integrations stay ignored and advisory.
intent.latitude: Choose issue grouping by implementation dependency and commit after each milestone.
intent.escalation: Stop if a fix requires tracker-specific core semantics or broad planning schema redesign beyond the issue acceptance.

execution.next_step: Commit and push #333/#334, close those issues with proof, then archive this completed plan.
execution.proof: Focused package tests, summary/checker, ruff, and dogfood commands.

invariants: Core package behavior remains external-provider agnostic.
Completed execplans do not pass as clean live planning state.
Compact TOML state is exercised as a primary path.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make package dogfooding findings become product behavior rather than one-off cleanup

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue through #332-#336 until all are closed or explicitly rerouted

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest issues and implement them end-to-end.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: use the package and local integration to implement the fresh issues created from dogfooding findings, with proof, commits, pushes, and closures
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: start with #332/#335, then #336, then #333/#334 provider-agnostic reconciliation/current-vs-historical reporting
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject tracker-specific core behavior and reject leaving stale planning residue live

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: stay on master; core package must be provider-agnostic; use package workflow; commit/push after milestones
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact test fixtures, helper names, and issue grouping by dependency
delegated_judgment.escalate when: a feature requires hard-coding a host tracker or changing repo-owned planning authority

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning/workspace package source and tests, active planning state and execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused pytest for touched behavior; ruff for touched Python; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before broad schema redesign or tracker-specific package core
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated memory behavior or branch state

context_budget.recoverable later: archived historical plans and broad #230 product-compression direction
context_budget.externalize before shift: update execution_run, proof_report, intent_satisfaction, and closure_check after each milestone
context_budget.pre-work config pull: local Codex snapshot, agentic-workspace preflight/summary, gh issue list
context_budget.tiny resumability note: implement package hygiene issues from dogfooding in dependency order
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after each commit/push milestone or if provider-agnostic reconciliation scope grows

stop_conditions.stop when: #332-#336 are implemented, validated, committed, pushed, closed, and the plan is archived or remaining work is explicitly routed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: hard-coded GitHub semantics appear necessary in shipped package core
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work becomes broad product simplification under #230
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: summary/checker or focused tests fail after attempted fixes

immediate_next_action: Commit and push the #333/#334 reconcile milestone.
Close #333 and #334 with proof, then archive this completed plan.
invariants: Checked-in planning state remains the active authority.
Local integration remains ignored and advisory.
validation_commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q -k "archive or planning_surface_health or completed"
completion_criteria: #332 exposes completed live execplans as planning-surface attention.
#335 archive-plan refusal messages name accepted values and field paths.
#336 compact-state dogfooding coverage protects package commands.
#333 provider-agnostic reconcile/report exists or is honestly scoped and proven.
#334 current external-work signals are separated from historical audit references without GitHub assumptions.

execution_run.handoff source: active execplan plus local Codex snapshot
execution_run.what happened: Implemented #332/#335, #336, and #333/#334: completed live execplans now surface archive_accumulation_drift, archive-plan refusal details name accepted closeout values, compact TOML state is dogfooded through summary/promote/archive, provider-agnostic reconcile is available through agentic-planning-bootstrap and agentic-workspace, and intent validation now separates current external-work state from historical audit references.
execution_run.scope touched: planning installer and CLI, workspace CLI command manifest/contracts, tests, and active planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "archive_execplan or planning_summary_exposes_closure_evidence or planning_surface_health"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "dogfoods_compact_state or promote_todo_item_to_execplan or archive_execplan or planning_summary_exposes_closure_evidence or planning_surface_health"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "intent_validation or planning_reconcile or cli_reconcile or workspace_reconcile or workspace_summary_json_defaults or cli_commands or defaults_command"; uv run ruff check packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #333 and #334 are ready to close after commit/push; then archive this completed plan.
execution_run.next step: Commit and push #333/#334, close those issues, then archive the completed plan.

finished_run_review.intent served: yes for implementation; issue closure and plan archive remain as closeout steps
finished_run_review.config compliance: followed compact summary/report/reconcile/checker workflow and stayed on master per user correction
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #333/#334 after push, then archive this plan

proof_report.validation proof: Focused pytest, ruff, contract tooling checker, planning checker, workspace reconcile dogfood output, compact summary dogfood output, and git diff whitespace check passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: validation commands listed in execution_run.validations run

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement the freshly ingested package dogfooding issues end-to-end.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep agent work restartable, reviewable, compact, and grounded in structured local surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Turn dogfooding friction into package behavior and regression coverage.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does the package make stale planning state and provider-specific assumptions harder to miss?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the implementation is complete but issue closure and plan archive are still pending
closure_check.evidence carried forward: execution_run and proof_report list the implemented surfaces and validations
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen if provider-specific assumptions enter core reconcile behavior or compact summary hides current external-work state

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Implemented #332-#336 package dogfooding follow-through behavior through the final #333/#334 reconcile milestone.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused tests, ruff, contract tooling checker, planning checker, reconcile dogfood output, summary dogfood output, and git diff check passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: close #333/#334 and archive this execplan after the milestone commit is pushed
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Provider-agnostic reconcile and current-vs-historical external work separation are now covered by package and workspace CLI tests.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted #332-#336 after local Codex integration setup and issue ingestion.

2026-04-26: Completed #336 package CLI compact-state dogfooding coverage.
2026-04-26: Completed #333/#334 provider-agnostic reconcile and current-vs-historical external-work separation.
